title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Interviews with leaders, academics, visionaries and entrepreneurs whose work is changing the world. Offers insight into those who are applying their skills to solve challenges at a local, regional and international level.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Wagyu captivates food lovers worldwide with fine marbling, melt-in-the-mouth texture, and rich aroma. Join us as we explore strict cattle management and the dishes that highlight wagyu's appeal.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: Visiting an Edo-style open-air teahouse, where the townspeople enjoy refreshments, conversation, and the company of exotic birds. For them, these outings were about more than a good time.

title: Science View
subTitle: Highlighting the cutting edge research, innovation, and technology being created by Japanese scientists and engineers.

title: Frontrunners
subTitle: Social entrepreneur Doya Yuto supports Ghana through his fashion brand and NPO. By providing fair wages and reinvesting profits, he builds schools and expands education in disadvantaged communities.

title: Core Kyoto
subTitle: An exploration of how the ancient capital of Kyoto continues to provide a source of innovation after 1200 years.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Japanese yams are a traditional ingredient loved for their sticky texture. Experience a harvest using heavy machinery to dig deep underground, then savor dishes including tororo over rice.

title: A Second Disaster: 15 Years After the Tsunami
subTitle: In a town nearly destroyed by the 2011 tsunami, fishermen who helped rebuild now face a second disaster as climate change and warmer seas threaten their future.

title: Dive in Tokyo
subTitle: Okachimachi is an area just north of Akihabara renowned for its jewelry district. Its story involves Buddhist craftspeople, American servicemen, and more recently, communities of foreign artisans.

title: Japanology Plus
subTitle: Once symbols of Japan's postwar growth, danchi housing complexes now face aging buildings and residents. We look at new initiatives that are reshaping and even revitalizing these communities.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Ito Junji is a horror manga artist renowned worldwide for his intricate artwork and unique vision, which earned him a place in the prestigious Eisner Awards Hall of Fame in 2025. What inspires him?

title: Japan's Top Inventions
subTitle: Nikki Tsukamoto Kininmonth learns about heat-resistant labels and meets Takehisa Kenji, the CEO of a company that manufactures these labels.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Japanese yams are a traditional ingredient loved for their sticky texture. Experience a harvest using heavy machinery to dig deep underground, then savor dishes including tororo over rice.

title: Kabuki Kool
subTitle: The historic figure of Minamoto no Yoshitsune has been immortalised in many kabuki plays as a child, a skilled warrior, and a tragic hero. Explore with actor Kataoka Ainosuke.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: Every round of sumo wrestling requires a sacred ritual to expel the evil spirits, including drinking water to cleanse and purify their bodies.

title: Outside In Lost in Academia
subTitle: Japanese culture is in the global spotlight like never before. Dive into the work of international scholars exploring Japan in bold, original ways, uncovering unique perspectives and deep insights.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Michie Yasufuso, a Brazilian of Japanese descent, works in Kanagawa Prefecture with foreign residents in Japan and helps them learn Japanese.The tour, we will speak with Yasufuso about Japan's challenges and the shape of a multicultural society.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: The key to tasty kiwi is a ripening process that takes place after harvest. Discovering new varieties that are being developed in Kagawa, and feasting one's eyes on innovative dishes.

subTitle: Showcasing a New Year's concert at a samurai residence where a master koto player delights a crowd of gorgeously dressed celebrity-types, and lounging in the best seat in the house is no ordinary VIP.

subTitle: Kogawa Nobuko and Kogawa Makoto pick camellias in their yard before demonstrating how to make camellia liquor and salt.

subTitle: Facing struggles since the Hokuriku Shinkansen's Kanazawa extension, Niigata's Echigo Tokimeki Railway and Hokuetsu Express make valiant efforts to survive.

title: Instruments of a Beating Heart
subTitle: First graders in a Tokyo public elementary school are presented with a challenge for the final semester. Examining the Japanese educational system's tenuous balance between self-sacrifice and human growth as it teaches the next generation.

subTitle: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Sake brewer Noguchi Naohiko shares his philosophy on balancing ancient tradition with modern innovation, forged over seven decades in the industry.

title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World
subTitle: Each year, 20 million people are displaced by climate change. If nothing is done, these climate refugees will number 216 million by 2050. We examine footage of problem areas and the latest research.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: Summer is winding down, and tonight down by Edo Bay the streets are filled with professional entertainers, food sellers, and revelers. It's getting late, but this party is just getting started.

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Japanese researchers are uncovering more and more of matcha's benefits, from improving sleep and cognitive function to easing depression and even boosting muscle mass.

title: Young Engineers Decommissioning
subTitle: With safety as the top priority, the work to fully decommission the Fukushima Daiichi facility continues, with approximately 3,600 and 5,000 employees engaging in each day's operations.
